text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Known acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4 morphology, newly diagnosed. CT requested for initial disease staging and systemic workup: evaluation for splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, lymphadenopathy, extramedullary leukemic infiltration (granulocytic sarcoma/chloroma), and assessment for occult infectious source prior to initiation of induction chemotherapy. WBC at presentation 87,400/microliter with circulating blasts.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in a single breath-hold acquisition following administration of 100 mL of nonionic intravenous contrast (iohexol) with portal venous phase imaging of the abdomen and pelvis. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reconstructions generated. Oral contrast was not administered given the urgent clinical indication.

COMPARISON:
No prior imaging available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal airspace consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pulmonary nodules identified. No interstitial infiltrates or interlobular septal thickening. The airways are patent to the level of the subsegmental bronchi bilaterally. No endobronchial lesions. No evidence of pulmonary infection or leukemic pulmonary infiltration.

Pleural Spaces: No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax. The pleural surfaces appear smooth and unremarkable bilaterally.

Mediastinum: The mediastinum is midline. No mediastinal widening or soft tissue masses. The thymus is not enlarged and shows expected fatty involution appropriate for patient age. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy by size criteria; all visualized mediastinal nodes are well within normal size limits (largest prevascular node 6 mm short axis). No anterior mediastinal mass to suggest leukemic thymic involvement.

Heart and Pericardium: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. The pericardium is intact without effusion. No intracardiac thrombus or mass is appreciated, though this is not a dedicated cardiac study.

Chest Wall and Musculature: No soft tissue masses or abnormal soft tissue densities within the chest wall. No evidence of leukemic chest wall infiltration.

Osseous Structures (Chest): Vertebral bodies and posterior elements of the thoracic spine are intact. No focal lytic or sclerotic lesions identified. Cortical margins are well preserved. No pathologic fractures. The ribs and clavicles are intact bilaterally. Note is made that osseous structures are not optimally evaluated without dedicated bone windows on routine soft tissue CT; however, no obvious abnormality is appreciated.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size, with a craniocaudal span of approximately 15.1 cm in the midclavicular line (within normal limits). The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous attenuation without focal lesions, masses, or areas of abnormal enhancement. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. No periportal lymphadenopathy. The liver margins are smooth. No subcapsular hematoma or hepatic laceration.

Spleen: The spleen is mildly enlarged, measuring approximately 14.2 cm in greatest craniocaudal dimension (upper limit of normal approximately 12-13 cm). The splenic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesions, infarcts, or abnormal enhancement. The splenic capsule is intact. The splenic vein is patent and of normal caliber. This degree of splenomegaly is consistent with splenic sequestration and/or leukemic involvement in the setting of newly diagnosed AML with marked leukocytosis; no discrete focal splenic masses to suggest leukemic deposits (chloromas).

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size and morphology. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic fluid or soft tissue stranding. No focal pancreatic lesions.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and position, with smooth contours and symmetric corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis. No nephrolithiasis or calcification. No perirenal fluid or masses. The ureters are not dilated as visualized.

Adrenal Glands: The bilateral adrenal glands are of normal size and configuration. No adrenal nodules or masses.

Biliary System: The gallbladder is present and distended with bile, no gallstones or wall thickening. The common bile duct measures 4 mm and is not dilated.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The visualized bowel loops are normal in caliber and wall thickness. No focal bowel wall thickening, pneumatosis, or bowel obstruction. The appendix is visualized and appears normal (non-distended, measuring <6 mm). No mesenteric fat stranding or free air.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta and its major branches are patent without evidence of stenosis or aneurysmal dilation. The celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric arteries appear widely patent. Portal and splenic veins are patent with normal flow characteristics by enhancement pattern.

Lymph Nodes (Abdomen): No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes identified in the retroperitoneum, mesentery, or porta hepatis. The largest visualized retroperitoneal node measures approximately 7 mm in short axis, within normal size criteria. No bulky adenopathy or lymph node conglomerates.

Peritoneum and Ascites: No free intraperitoneal fluid. The peritoneal surfaces are smooth without nodularity or thickening to suggest leukemic infiltration.

Soft Tissue (Abdomen): No soft tissue masses, chloromatous deposits, or abnormal soft tissue infiltration identified within the abdominal compartment. No abdominal wall masses.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen/Pelvis): The visualized vertebral bodies of the lumbar spine and sacrum demonstrate maintained height and alignment without discrete focal lytic or blastic lesions. The cortical margins appear intact. The iliac crests, pubic symphysis, and ischial tuberosities are without focal destructive changes. The femoral heads and proximal femora, as visualized, are unremarkable. No periosteal reaction identified.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is normally distended and smooth-walled. No intravesical masses or filling defects. No perivesical soft tissue abnormality.

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus is normal in size and morphology for a premenopausal female. The endometrium and myometrium are unremarkable. The ovaries are bilaterally visualized and appear normal without masses, cysts, or enlargement. No adnexal pathology.

Rectum and Sigmoid Colon: No rectal or sigmoid wall thickening. No pelvic fat stranding to suggest perirectal or presacral inflammatory or infectious process.

Lymph Nodes (Pelvis): No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. The iliac chains bilaterally demonstrate no enlarged nodes.

Muscles and Soft Tissue (Pelvis): No psoas muscle enlargement, edema, or abscess. No iliopsoas collection. No pelvic floor or perineal masses. No chloromatous soft tissue masses in the pelvis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Mild splenomegaly with the spleen measuring 14.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension. The parenchyma is homogeneous and no focal splenic lesions are identified. In the clinical context of newly diagnosed AML with marked leukocytosis and circulating blasts, this finding is most consistent with leukemic involvement or reactive splenomegaly from hematopoietic stress. No definite focal chloromatous deposits within the spleen.

2. No hepatomegaly. The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesions.

3. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, axillae, retroperitoneum, pelvis, or inguinal regions. No lymphadenopathy to suggest extramedullary leukemic spread via the lymphatic system.

4. No pulmonary consolidation, nodules, or ground-glass opacities. No imaging evidence of superimposed pulmonary infection or infiltrate at this time.

5. No discrete soft tissue masses, chloromatous deposits, or other extramedullary leukemic lesions (granulocytic sarcomas) identified in any compartment on this examination.

6. No lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified on the osseous structures included in this examination; however, dedicated bone imaging is more sensitive for marrow-based disease.

7. No evidence of occult deep soft tissue infection, abscess, or other infectious source identified.

Overall, the CT findings are most consistent with mild splenomegaly attributable to the known AML diagnosis with no imaging evidence of extramedullary leukemic disease or granulocytic sarcoma formation. These findings support proceeding with planned systemic induction chemotherapy.

Dictated and electronically signed by: Attending Radiologist, Diagnostic Radiology / Oncologic Imaging
